segments.text: The song of songs, which is Solomon's.
Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth—
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: For your love is better than wine.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: Because of the fragrance of your good ointments, Your name is ointment poured forth; Therefore the virgins love you.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: Draw me away! We will run after you.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: The king has brought me into his chambers.
We will be glad and rejoice in you. We will remember your love more than wine. Rightly do they love you.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: I am dark, but lovely, O daughters of Jerusalem, Like the tents of Kedar, Like the curtains of Solomon.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: Do not look upon me, because I am dark, Because the sun has tanned me. My mother's sons were angry with me; They made me the keeper of the vineyards, But my own vineyard I have not kept.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: Tell me, O you whom I love, Where you feed your flock, Where you make it rest at noon. For why should I be as one who veils herself By the flocks of your companions?
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: If you do not know, O fairest among women, Follow in the footsteps of the flock, And feed your little goats Beside the shepherds' tents.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: I have compared you, my love, To my filly among Pharaoh's chariots.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Your cheeks are lovely with ornaments, Your neck with chains of gold.
segments.speaker: The Daughters of Jerusalem

segments.text: We will make you ornaments of gold With studs of silver.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: While the king is at his table, My spikenard sends forth its fragrance.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: A bundle of myrrh is my beloved to me, That lies all night between my breasts.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: My beloved is to me a cluster of henna blooms In the vineyards of En Gedi.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Behold, you are fair, my love! Behold, you are fair! You have dove's eyes.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: Behold, you are handsome, my beloved! Yes, pleasant! Also our bed is green.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: The beams of our houses are cedar, And our rafters of fir.
